description: AwsSmBackend and AzureKvBackend can be constructed via from_uri() with a ?field=<name> query parameter. When constructed this way, get() returns only the extracted JSON field — but put() always returns SecretError::InvalidUri at runtime because overwriting only one field of a JSON object is not implemented. The problem: from_uri() succeeds, WritableSecretStore is implemented, and no static or construction-time error is returned. Callers who build a SecretStore from a URI string at startup and only call put() at rotation time will discover this failure at the worst possible moment. The contract of WritableSecretStore is that put() stores the value; receiving InvalidUri from put() violates the trait's implicit contract. What goes wrong: a library user constructs a writable backend with ?field=token, stores it, and calls put() during credential rotation — receiving a confusing InvalidUri error with no indication this was predictable at construction time. Fix options: (a) return InvalidUri from from_uri() when ?field= is present and a writable backend is requested, or (b) document this clearly on from_uri() not just on put(), or (c) omit WritableSecretStore impl when ?field= is set. Affects: secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs and secretx-azure-kv/src/lib.rs.
title: ?field= on writable backends silently poisons put()
